records.ety: [OE. bicumen, becumen, AS. becuman to come to, to happen; akin to D. bekomen, OHG.a piquëman, Goth. biquiman to come upon, G. bekommen to get, suit. See Be-, and Come.]
records.morph: [imp. Became (<?/); p. p. Become; p. pr. & vb. n. Becoming.]

defs: To pass from one state to another; to enter into some state or condition, by a change from another state, or by assuming or receiving new properties or qualities, additional matter, or a new character.
quotes: The Lord God . . . breathed into his nostrils the breath of life; and man became a living soul.
That error now which is become my crime.
But, madam, where is Warwick then become!
What is then become of so huge a multitude?

defs: To suit or be suitable to; to be congruous with; to befit; to accord with, in character or circumstances; to be worthy of, or proper for; to cause to appear well; -- said of persons and things.
quotes: It becomes me so to speak of so excellent a poet.
I have known persons so anxious to have their dress become them, as to convert it, at length, into their proper self, and thus actually to become the dress.
